ci_support_linux_64_mpimpich.yaml:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
fortran_compiler:
- gfortran
fortran_compiler_version:
- '14'
libcblas:
- 3.9.* *netlib
mpi:
- mpich
mpich:
- '5'
openmpi:
- '5'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
  - fortran_compiler_version

about.description:
SPLA provides specialized functions for distributed matrix multiplication,
as required in some computational material science codes. It aims for
maximum computation and communication overlap when possible and allows any
combination of host and device pointers if compiled with GPU support. C++, C
and Fortran interfaces are available.
about.summary: Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) library, tailored for computational material science codes.
Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) shared library, tailored for computational material science codes.
Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) static library, tailored for computational material science codes.

raw_meta_yaml:
{% set name = "spla" %}
{% set version = "1.6.1" %}

{% set build = 0 %}

{% set mpi = mpi or "openmpi" %}
{% set mpi_prefix = "mpi_" + mpi %}

package:
  name: {{ name|lower }}
  version: "{{ version }}"

source:
  url: https://github.com/eth-cscs/spla/archive/refs/tags/v{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 62b51e6ce05c41cfc1c6f6600410f9549a209c50f0331e1db41047f94493e02f

build:
  number: {{ build }}
  skip: True  # [win]

outputs:
  - name: spla
    script: build-spla.sh
    build:
      string: {{ mpi_prefix }}_h{{ PKG_HASH }}_{{ build }}
      run_exports:
        - {{ pin_subpackage("spla", max_pin="x.x") }}
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler("c") }}
        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
        - {{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - {{ compiler("fortran") }}
        - {{ mpi }}
        - cmake >=3.18
        - ninja
        - pkg-config
      host:
        - {{ mpi }}
        - libcblas
      run:
        - {{ mpi }}
        - libcblas
    test:
      commands:
        - test -f "${PREFIX}/lib/libspla${SHLIB_EXT}"
    about:
      summary: Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) shared library, tailored for computational material science codes.
  - name: spla-static
    script: build-spla.sh
    build:
      string: {{ mpi_prefix }}_h{{ PKG_HASH }}_{{ build }}
      run_exports:
        - {{ pin_subpackage("spla-static", max_pin="x.x") }}
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler("c") }}
        - {{ stdlib("c") }}
        - {{ compiler("cxx") }}
        - {{ compiler("fortran") }}
        - {{ mpi }}
        - cmake >=3.18
        - ninja
        - pkg-config
      host:
        - {{ mpi }}
        - libcblas
      run:
        - {{ mpi }}
        - libcblas
    test:
      commands:
        - test -f "${PREFIX}/lib/libspla.a"
    about:
      summary: Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) static library, tailored for computational material science codes.

about:
  home: https://github.com/AdhocMan/spla
  license: BSD-3-Clause
  license_family: BSD
  license_file: LICENSE
  summary: Specialized Linear Algebra (SpLA) library, tailored for computational material science codes.
  description: |
    SPLA provides specialized functions for distributed matrix multiplication,
    as required in some computational material science codes. It aims for
    maximum computation and communication overlap when possible and allows any
    combination of host and device pointers if compiled with GPU support. C++, C
    and Fortran interfaces are available.
  doc_url: https://spla.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
  dev_url: https://github.com/eth-cscs/spla

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - AdhocMan
    - ltalirz
    - mkrack
